title: How the Council of the EU dealt with a request for public access to documents concerning the language regime in the EU
text: Dear Mr X, You submitted a complaint to the European Ombudsman about how the Council of the EU dealt with a request for public access to documents concerning the language regime in the EU. The Ombudsman opened an inquiry into the Council’s refusal to give public access and requested to inspect the documents at issue. The Council has now informed the Ombudsman that, in the context of this inquiry, it has carried out a new assessment of the three documents at issue in your confirmatory application. As a result, the Council has decided to grant full access to the three documents. You remain dissatisfied with the Council’s confirmatory decision to refuse access to the documents. Please note that the Ombudsman’s inquiry concerned the refusal to grant public access to the documents. Given that the Council has reassessed its position and has now granted full access to the three documents, the case is closed with the conclusion that it has been settled . Yours sincerely, Rosita Hickey Director of Inquiries Strasbourg, 20/02/2026
